name: Adams Courts
description: Adams Courts is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in North Canaan, CT. There are 2 outdoor asphalt courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts are private. Amenities include restrooms.
open_play_schedule: Bring your own players. For North Canaan residents and their guests only.

name: Connecticut Sports Arena
description: Come play pickleball at Connecticut Sports Arena in New Milford, CT! There are 4 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A one-time fee is required to play. The courts can be reserved. Players have access to lights, food, locker rooms, restrooms, and water.
fees: Fee required — Court rentals to groups

name: Hale YMCA Youth & Family Center
description: Hale YMCA Youth & Family Center is a place to play pickleball in Torrington, CT. There are 2 indoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The facility includes amenities like restrooms. A membership is required to play.

name: Harwinton Conservation/Recreation Area
description: Harwinton Conservation/Recreation Area is a place to play pickleball in Harwinton, CT. There are 3 outdoor courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The courts are private.
hours: Monday - Wednesday, 8:30am - 4:00pm, Thursday, 8:30am - 5:00pm, Friday, 8:30am - 12:30pm Quick Links Calendar Emergency Services FAQs News Town Clerk /QuickLinks.aspx Site Links Home Site Map Contact Us Accessibility Copyright Notices Privacy Policy /QuickLinks.aspx Government Websites by CivicPlus® Loading Loading Do Not Show Again Close Arrow Left Arrow Right [] Slideshow Left Arrow Slideshow Right Arrow

name: Harwinton Recreation Department
description: Come play pickleball at Harwinton Recreation Department in Harwinton, CT! There are 4 outdoor courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. The courts are free.

name: Lake Winfield Recreation Area
description: Lake Winfield Recreation Area is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Plymouth, CT. There are 4 outdoor asphalt courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free.

name: New Milford Tennis & Swim Club
description: New Milford Tennis & Swim Club is a place to play pickleball in New Milford, CT. There are 5 outdoor clay courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. Reservations are available. Players have access to restrooms, a pro shop or equipment store, and water. A membership is required to play.

name: Pinewoods Health & Racquet Club
description: Round Robins with a Pro for Advanced Beginners and Intermediate Players; Clinics for Beginners; Private and Semiprivate lessons
open_play_schedule: Summer hours; Fall/Winter hours expand in Sept.

name: Rowley Park Pickleball
description: Rowley Park Pickleball is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Winsted, CT. There are 2 outdoor hard courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The facility includes amenities like restrooms, water, and lights. The courts are free.

name: Sharon Country Club
description: Sharon Country Club is a place to play pickleball in Sharon, CT. There are 2 outdoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A membership is required to play. Amenities include restrooms.

name: The Twin Lakes Beach Club
description: Come play pickleball at The Twin Lakes Beach Club in Salisbury, CT! There are 2 outdoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The facility includes amenities like restrooms and lights. A membership is required to play. Reservations are available.

name: Walnut Hill Community Church
description: Walnut Hill Community Church is a place to play pickleball in New Milford, CT. There are 3 indoor wood courts. Tape lines have been laid, and portable nets are available. The courts are private. Amenities include locker rooms, restrooms, trainers or lessons, and lights. You can reserve the courts.
fees: Private — Reservations through CourtReserve app. Search Walnut Hill $5 fee to play
